summary: Filter-style remove-duplicates on an i64 slice with two tail-recursive helpers (count_at counting occurrences, build_at appending elements whose total count = 1 via extend_from_slice with a typed [i64;1] chunk); the outer build_at calls count_at *inside* its body, so the strong-induction proof generalizes on `size − i.toNat`, cases on the inner RustM result (ok/fail/div), and propagates fail/div via dedicated build_at_step_count_fail/div lemmas to contradict the outer RustM.ok hypothesis — the spec is a List-of-surviving-indices `build_indices : Nat → List Nat` (rather than the usual recursive Nat→Int oracle) with the output invariant phrased as `acc.val[k] = numbers[build_indices.getD k 0]`, requiring two custom getD-on-append bridge lemmas (list_getD_append_left, list_getD_append_singleton_at_len) and List.pairwise_iff_getElem to derive strict monotonicity; three top-level theorems (order-preservation existential / soundness / completeness) each project one conjunct of a shared build_at_correct_aux lemma, with completeness flowing through `build_indices_mem` and `List.mem_iff_getElem`.
